body:
All --

We have a FERC request for information on Enron's procedural participation in California wholesale electricity market processes during 2000-2001, with a December 15 submission deadline. We need to coordinate the response across government affairs and regulatory compliance functions now, before we run out of review time.

Here's the division of labor I'm proposing. I want pushback from legal on the assignments before we lock this in.

**Government Affairs (Dasovich / Shapiro / Steffes)**
We take ownership of the policy and procedural participation sub-questions -- the ones covering Enron's engagement with ISO market design processes, stakeholder meetings, working group participation, and any formal submissions or comments filed during that period. Our documentation package will pull from the government affairs files: lobbying disclosure records, meeting logs, and any correspondence with ISO or PX staff on market structure matters.

**Regulatory Compliance (Hain + coordinated with Fossum)**
Mary, I think you and Drew should own the sub-questions covering compliance with FERC orders, market monitoring obligations, and the specific procedural filings Enron made before the Commission during 2000-2001. Documentation should include the actual filings, any deficiency responses, and the related correspondence with FERC staff.

**Legal Review (Sanders + outside counsel)**
Richard, legal should hold review authority over the full draft before it goes out. Given the current environment, I want a senior legal sign-off before we file anything. We should also flag any areas where privilege or work product concerns apply.

**Proposed Timeline**
- November 19: First drafts due to Steffes from GA and Regulatory Compliance
- November 26: Consolidated draft to legal for review
- December 3: Legal comments back
- December 6: Internal reconciliation call (all parties)
- December 10: Final draft to outside counsel
- December 13: Outside counsel review complete
- December 14: Final Enron sign-off
- December 15: FERC filing

If anyone has conflicts with this schedule or thinks the division of labor needs adjustment, please flag today. I want to get a kickoff call scheduled for this Thursday to get the teams moving.

Jim

Jim --

Understood. The regulatory compliance bucket is workable, and I'm prepared to take ownership of the FERC order compliance, market monitoring obligations, and procedural filings sub-questions. Documentation package will pull from the compliance files -- the actual filings, deficiency responses, and related FERC staff correspondence during 2000-2001.

A couple of points on the division of labor I want to flag before we lock it:

First, on Drew Fossum -- Drew is VP and General Counsel for Enron Transportation Services (Northern Natural Gas and Transwestern pipeline operations). He's not a natural fit for the California market compliance sub-questions. I'd recommend we treat this as a legal coordination matter rather than an ownership role for Drew. If we need senior legal input on the regulatory compliance section, Richard Sanders is the right counterpart.

Second, on privilege and work product -- Richard should flag any areas early in the process. Given the current environment, we should be deliberate about what goes into the working draft versus what stays in a separate privileged file. I'd suggest Richard identify those areas before November 26 so we don't have to reconstruct sections.

The timeline is workable on my end. One flag: if FERC staff correspondence or deficiency responses require retrieval from archived files, we may need a day or two of buffer on the November 19 draft date. I'll confirm by Wednesday whether that's a constraint.

I'm available Thursday for the kickoff call. I'll come prepared with a preliminary outline of the compliance sub-questions and the documentation inventory so we can scope the drafts efficiently.

Mary

All --

Mary's corrections are well-taken. On Drew Fossum -- you're right, and I stand corrected. Drew is VP and General Counsel for Enron Transportation Services. He is not the right owner for the California market compliance sub-questions. The revised structure should be:

**Regulatory Compliance** -- Hain owns the sub-questions on FERC order compliance, market monitoring obligations, and Enron's procedural filings before the Commission during 2000-2001. Documentation package as Mary described.

**Legal Coordination** -- Richard Sanders is the appropriate counterpart for senior legal input on the regulatory compliance section. Given that Richard is already active on FERC-related matters -- I see the October 2001 FERC California Report work in recent threads -- he has the relevant context to engage efficiently on this response.

On privilege and work product: Richard, Mary is right that we need your flag on those areas before November 26. Please identify any sections that should be held in a separate privileged file by November 22 at the latest so we can scope the working draft accordingly. That gives us a few days of buffer before the consolidated draft goes to legal.

Mary -- I'll hold to November 19 for your preliminary outline and documentation inventory unless you flag by Wednesday that the archive retrieval is a constraint. If it is, we can build in a day or two of buffer on the front end and compress the legal review window slightly if needed.

I'll send a calendar hold for Thursday for the kickoff call. Richard, I'll loop you in on that invite directly.

Revised ownership summary for the record:
- **Government Affairs (Dasovich / Shapiro / Steffes)** -- policy and procedural participation sub-questions; ISO market design engagement; stakeholder meetings; working group participation; formal submissions and comments. Documentation: lobbying disclosure records, meeting logs, ISO/PX staff correspondence on market structure.
- **Regulatory Compliance (Hain)** -- FERC order compliance; market monitoring obligations; Enron's procedural filings before the Commission during 2000-2001. Documentation: actual filings, deficiency responses, FERC staff correspondence.
- **Legal Review (Sanders + outside counsel)** -- review authority over full draft; privilege and work product flagging by November 22; senior legal sign-off before filing.

Jim

Confirmed. The revised ownership structure is locked in from my end.

Richard -- November 22 privilege flag is noted. I'll keep the working draft lean until your identification comes back, so we avoid reconstructing sections. On the archive retrieval question, I'll confirm by Wednesday. Barring a constraint there, November 19 stands.

Thursday kickoff works. I'll have the preliminary outline and documentation inventory ready.

Mary

All --

We're aligned. Ownership is locked, timeline stands, and the Thursday kickoff is scheduled.

Richard, your invite is going out shortly. Your primary action items: confirm availability for the kickoff, and flag privilege and work product areas by November 22 as discussed. If you have bandwidth concerns on the November 26 consolidated draft review, let me know now so we can plan accordingly.

Everyone else -- see you Thursday. Come ready to map the sub-questions to specific draft sections so we can move efficiently once the outline is set.

Jim
